questions.text: Which of the following is a rational number?
questions.explanation: 0 is a rational number because it can be expressed as 0/1. √2, π, and √3 are irrational numbers.

questions.text: The sum of a rational number and its additive inverse is always 0.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of a number is what you add to it to get zero. For any rational number a, a + (-a) = 0.
The reciprocal of a fraction is obtained by swapping the numerator and denominator. Reciprocal of 5/7 is 7/5.

questions.text: Between any two rational numbers, there are ________ rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Between any two rational numbers, there exist infinitely many rational numbers. This is a key property of rational numbers.
